description:
# Follow-on to .43 — leverage the matrix for correctness wins

## What changes

Once `pattern_matrix::compile` exists (.43.C), it has all the info to
detect:

1. **Unreachable clauses** — a row whose constructor is fully covered
   by earlier rows in every column it discriminates on.
2. **Non-exhaustive matches** — a `Decision` tree whose default branch
   is reachable but the function's input type doesn't include any
   value that would land there.

Emit these as warnings via the diagnostic infrastructure (fz-ul4.20).
CLAUDE.md mandates warnings-as-errors, so this becomes real correctness
enforcement.

## What stays

The matrix infrastructure built in .43.C-.H. This ticket reads from it,
doesn't modify it.

## What's removed

Nothing structural. Adds a new diagnostic site.

## Acceptance

- A test fixture with an unreachable clause produces the warning.
- A test fixture with an inexhaustive case (on a type the typer
  knows) produces the warning.
- Warnings render via the existing diagnostic renderer.
- All current fixtures pass (none have unreachable/inexhaustive
  clauses — if any do, that's a bug to fix, not a warning to
  suppress).

## Risk

Exhaustiveness checking against polymorphic descrs is subtle. Scope v1
to: warn when a case's default is unreachable AND no row body is
reachable from default. Skip the harder "input type can never reach
this row" check until we have a need.

## Dependencies

Blocked by .43.C (matrix types must exist).
title: fz-ul4.45: dead-clause + exhaustiveness warnings from pattern matrix
